body_txt: Beetles in fiction and folklore are sometimes used as symbols of thunder and lightning, if not given actual electric powers . Interestingly, this association seems to have arisen independently both in Europe and in East Asia. Stag beetles are the most likely to receive this treatment, with the European stag beetle , actually being referred to as "thunder beetle" in some cultures (see the Real Life section below). The firefly is another common candidate, with its ability to produce flashes of light leading some species to be nicknamed "lightning bugs". In Japan this is also common for kabutomushi (the Japanese rhinoceros beetle), owing to the popularity of that species . May or may not use their horns or mandibles as a Wave-Motion Tuning Fork . A Sister Trope to Scarab Power and Tough Beetles . Sub-Trope of Shock and Awe . Compare Electric Jellyfish . For other animal-and-element combinations, see also Earthy Tortoise , Fiery Lion , Hot Wings , and Fiery Salamander .
